title: Knots
body: In the context of B-splines and NURBS, knots are a sequence of parameter values that determine where and how the control points affect the curve. They are defined in the 1D parametric space as $\Xi = \{\xi_1, \xi_2, ..., \xi_{n+p+1}\}$. They are essentially the 'joints' of the curve. The spacing of the knots can be uniform or non-uniform, which gives NURBS its 'Non-Uniform' property and allows for more flexible and precise curves.
